title: Treacle tart
subtitle: British golden syrup tart with a crumb filling baked in a buttery shortcrust shell
excerpt: Classic British tart with a filling of golden syrup, fresh white breadcrumbs, and lemon baked in a buttery shortcrust shell. The filling sets to a soft, chewy consistency with a slight crust on top and a deep amber colour. Traditionally served warm with clotted cream or custard. The filling takes minutes to assemble; the pastry benefits from a 30-minute chill before rolling.
sourceNotes: Treacle tart is a British baked pudding that dates from the late nineteenth century, shortly after the introduction of Lyle's Golden Syrup (first produced 1883). Mrs Beeton's Book of Household Management (various editions, Project Gutenberg) includes golden syrup puddings consistent with the tart's filling. The combination of golden syrup, fresh breadcrumbs, and lemon baked in a shortcrust shell has been a fixed point in British home baking since that period.

content.text: A 30-minute pastry chill prevents shrinkage during blind-baking. The filling is two minutes of stirring. The key moment is judging
when the filling is properly set
: a slight dome, no wobble in the centre, and deep amber all over.

attrs.prepNote: day-old, torn into fine fresh breadcrumbs

content.text: Make the pastry: rub the cold butter into the flour and icing sugar until the mixture looks like coarse crumbs. Add the egg yolk, salt, and 1 to 2 teaspoons of cold water and mix until the dough just comes together. Flatten into a disc, wrap, and refrigerate for 30 minutes.

Heat the oven to 190°C conventional (170°C fan). Roll the chilled pastry on a lightly floured surface and use it to line the tart tin. Trim the edges. Prick the base all over with a fork. Line with baking paper, fill with baking beans or dried rice, and blind-bake for 15 minutes. Remove the paper and weights and bake for a further 5 minutes until the base is dry and pale gold.

Make the filling: warm the golden syrup gently in a saucepan until it flows easily. Remove from the heat and stir in the breadcrumbs, lemon zest, and lemon juice. Pour into the hot pastry case and return to the oven for 15 to 20 minutes until the filling is set and deep golden. Cool in the tin for 10 minutes before slicing. Serve warm with clotted cream or custard.
